behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure boundary is not just local wiring; it is whether diarization/speaker labeling from the speech-to-text model is correct. REAL-INFRA is present (real API), but LLM-DEP/ML-DEP is stronger: correctness depends on real model output content. MOCK-HIDE: a stub STT response with perfect speaker tags would hide real diarization failures. MULTI-STEP: only one model decision is required here, so Workflow is not required. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text focuses on preserving speaker labels in STT output, not on multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production STT API and assert transcript segments contain stable, correct speaker labels that map to attendee identities used by summarization.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior is parser logic on transcript text to structured fields; a single function/object test can reproduce failures. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: no real model output required for this specific extraction rule. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text is about 'Transcript parsing extracts ... into the summary input structure' and can be validated with fixed transcript strings and expected parsed objects.
behaviors.test_description: Provide transcript text fixtures with explicit assignment phrases and assert parser outputs exact assignee/task pairs in the summary input schema.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The core failure is omission by the summarizer model, not just deterministic plumbing. REAL-INFRA may exist, but model-output quality is the deciding factor. MOCK-HIDE: fixed mock summaries would always satisfy/violate by construction and miss real model variability. MULTI-STEP: one agentic decision (summary generation) is sufficient to expose the failure; no required multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text checks a single output condition on the live summary, not a sequence of multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use real summarization model calls on transcripts with known assignee-task pairs and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in the returned structured summary.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This describes deterministic routing logic once decisions are identified; failure can be reproduced at one local transformation boundary. REAL-INFRA: none needed. LLM-DEP: none required if identified decisions are given as input to router. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text specifically targets 'routes ... into the decisions section schema', which is local mapping behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Pass a parsed intermediate object containing identified decisions into the routing function and assert outputs populate `decisions` schema fields and leave narrative fields unchanged for those items.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is whether the model surfaces and places decisions correctly in generated output. REAL-INFRA alone is insufficient; content quality from model inference is the boundary. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic canned summaries hide extraction/placement misses from real model behavior. MULTI-STEP: single summarization decision is enough; no required multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text asks for placement in one live summary output, not multiple sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcripts containing explicit decision statements and assert those decisions are present under `decisions` and absent as only narrative mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The behavior spans segmentation orchestration, repeated processing per segment, and cross-segment merge coherence. REAL-INFRA may be involved, but the key risk is multiple agentic outputs composed over sequence. LLM-DEP: coherence of merged summary depends on real model outputs per segment. MULTI-STEP: explicitly requires sequential processing and merge across segments, i.e., multiple agentic steps in sequence. Tie-breaker evidence for higher level: behavior text says 'processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary,' which disqualifies single-step Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, execute production split->transcribe->summarize per segment, then validate merged output preserves cross-segment continuity and complete structured sections without duplication/loss.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This is end-to-end pipeline reliability across multiple stages and handoffs; local mocks can hide real breakage. REAL-INFRA: includes real upload/network/service execution. LLM-DEP: includes real STT/LLM outputs in pipeline. MULTI-STEP: explicitly a full sequence from upload through delivery with agentic stages; this is a workflow boundary. Rejection gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text explicitly requires the 'full pipeline ... completes' across multiple stages, not a single model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Run a production-like end-to-end test with a real meeting recording through upload, STT API, summarization model, and output delivery endpoint; assert successful completion, valid structured schema, and no manual retries/interventions.
